title: Intel suggests it was snubbed by Crimson Desert dev after reaching out "many times" about Arc GPUs company says it provided "early hardware, drivers, and engineering resources" to studio
summary: Intel blames Pearl Abyss for lack of Arc GPU in Crimson Desert, claiming multiple attempts at collaboration.
insight: Intel accused of poor communication with game developers about GPU integration.

title: New Fortnite 'Rivalry' includes chance to win RTX 5080 top five players will be awarded GPU hardware in Chapter 7 Season 2 competition
summary: Fortnite's new 'Rivalry' system offers real-life rewards, including a free RTX 5080, to top players. The competition is part of Chapter 7 Season 2. Players must win 'rivalries' in matches and rank among the top five globally.
insight: Competitive gamers may be motivated by real-life GPU rewards

title: Crimson Desert offers no support for Intel GPUs developer Pearl Abyss says 'please refer to the refund policy'
summary: Crimson Desert doesn't support Intel GPUs, prompting refunds from affected customers.
insight: Intel GPU users excluded from game compatibility, seek refunds instead.

title: AMD releases FSR 4.1 for RX 9000-series GPUs new update delivers better Ray Regeneration, finer upscaled detail, and higher FPS
summary: AMD releases FSR 4.1 for RX 9000-series GPUs, delivering improved ray tracing and upscaling. This update brings AMD's RDNA 4 GPUs to parity with Sony's PS5 Pro in terms of ray tracing quality and image sharpness. Games that support these features will benefit from enhanced visuals and performance.
insight: Enhanced gaming experience with improved visuals and performance.

title: Planned 10-gigawatt Softbank data center in Ohio might be the largest in the world will require a $33 billion natural gas plant, equivalent to nine nuclear reactors
summary: SoftBank plans a massive $60 billion AI data center in Ohio, spanning multiple phases. The facility will be one of the largest in the world. It requires a $33 billion natural gas plant.
insight: Massive data center demands massive energy infrastructure investment

title: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin reveals 51,600 satellite space data center plans Project Sunrise will operate in sun-synchronous orbits between 500 1,800km in altitude
summary: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin plans to launch 51,600 satellites for a space data center. The satellites will operate in sun-synchronous orbits between 500 and 1,800km in altitude. Project Sunrise aims to provide global connectivity and data storage.
insight: Revolutionizing global data storage and connectivity with massive satellite constellations

title: China develops new ultra-cold alloy that can reach -273 C without helium could enable compact cooling for superconducting quantum chips, military equipment, and beyond
summary: Chinese scientists developed an ultra-cold alloy that can reach -273 C without helium, enabling compact cooling for superconducting quantum chips, military equipment, and beyond.
insight: Revolutionizes cooling technology for sensitive, high-tech applications worldwide

title: Acer Predator XB273U F5 27-inch QHD 360 Hz gaming monitor review: Utilizing G-Sync Pulsar to deliver phenomenal blur reduction
summary: The Acer Predator XB273U F5 features a 27-inch QHD IPS screen with 360 Hz refresh rate, HDR500, and G-Sync Pulsar technology for reduced blur and improved gaming performance. It offers wide-gamut color and Adaptive-Sync support for a seamless gaming experience. The display's 360 Hz refresh rate and G-Sync Pulsar tech make it ideal for fast-paced games.
insight: Blurs are eliminated in high-speed gaming with G-Sync Pulsar

title: Supermicro employees accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China perps used a hairdryer to move serial numbers between real hardware and thousands of dummy servers
summary: Three Super Micro employees have been charged with smuggling Nvidia hardware to China. They allegedly used a hairdryer to swap serial numbers between real and dummy servers. The total value of the smuggled hardware is estimated at $2.5 billion.
insight: US tech security compromised by insider sabotage and theft.

title: Seller gets scammed as eBay customer returns $4,000 RTX 5090 with missing GPU core and memory modules fully working Zotac stripped of most valuable components and sent back
summary: An eBay seller was scammed when a returned GPU appeared intact but lacked critical internal components, making it difficult to detect without disassembly. The GPU in question was a high-value item, a $4,000 RTX 5090, and was sent back by the buyer stripped of its most valuable components. This highlights the risk of returning goods with hidden defects.
insight: Internal component manipulation can make counterfeit goods difficult to detect externally

title: Radxa AICore DX-M1M M.2 2242 low-power AI module delivers 25 TOPS of edge AI performance for just 3W of power
summary: Radxa's AICore DX-M1M M.2 module delivers 25 TOPS AI performance at 3W power, suitable for industrial edge AI applications.
insight: Ultra-low power AI computing accelerates industrial edge AI adoption.

title: Certified Android devices won t let users sideload APK app files anymore, or at least it won t be straightforward
summary: Google will require developer verification for sideloading APK files on certified Android devices, starting in September 2026. This involves paying a fee, agreeing to terms, and providing identification. The process will be complex, but still possible for power users.
insight: Android users will need verification to sideload apps, limiting flexibility.

title: What s going on with Edge AI? And why a Fierce Sensors survey?
summary: A Fierce survey aims to uncover insights on Edge AI pilot success. Engineers are invited to share their experiences with Edge AI pilot projects. The survey hopes to identify challenges that prevent pilots from reaching production.
insight: Edge AI pilots fail due to insufficient data and testing.

title: Uber commits up to $1.25 billion in Rivian to deploy 10,000 robotaxis
summary: Uber commits up to $1.25 billion to deploy 10,000 Rivian robotaxis, gaining strategic benefits.
insight: Autonomous technology partnerships drive innovation and financial stability.

title: Nvidia "confirms" DLSS 5 relies on 2D frame data as testing reveals hallucinations
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 relies on 2D frame data, not underlying geometry, according to Nvidia's statements. This contradicts claims that the technology is not a post-processing filter. Testing has revealed hallucinations as a result.
insight: DLSS 5's limitations expose its post-processing filter nature

title: Google isn't backing away from Pentagon AI work, it's doubling down
summary: Google DeepMind is doubling down on national security AI work, despite controversy.
insight: Escalating national security ties may compromise Google's values and public image.

title: Reworked Apple Watch avoids ban, but Masimo battle escalates
summary: Apple's revised Apple Watch avoids ban after court ruling. The decision concludes that Apple's implementation falls outside Masimo's asserted rights.
insight: Patent dispute resolution may affect future wearable technology development

title: AMD releases Adrenaline 26.3.1 driver, adding FSR 4.1 support for Radeon RX 9000 GPUs
summary: AMD releases Adrenaline 26.3.1 driver with FSR 4.1 support for Radeon RX 9000 GPUs, improving image reconstruction quality and delivering sharper visuals.
insight: Enhanced image quality for older games and machine learning upscaling.

title: Android sideloading remains, but Google introduces stricter "advanced flow" process
summary: Google won't retire Android sideloading, but will make it more difficult. The advanced flow process is designed to protect users from potential threats. This change aims to balance user freedom with security.
insight: Android's sideloading balance shifts from accessibility to security emphasis.

title: Quantum cryptography pioneers win Turing Award for unhackable encryption breakthrough
summary: A pair of pioneers in quantum cryptography won the Turing Award for their work on the BB84 protocol, an encryption method that relies on the fragile nature of photons.
insight: Unhackable encryption breakthrough secures sensitive information with quantum science.

title: Crimson Desert won't work on Intel Arc GPUs, developer suggests users ask for refunds
summary: Crimson Desert's underwhelming performance has led to a significant drop in Pearl Abyss' stock price, indicating investor disappointment. The game's lack of compatibility with Intel Arc GPUs has been cited as a major issue. Developer suggests users consider refunds due to this problem.
insight: Intel Arc GPU incompatibility hurts Crimson Desert's market appeal significantly.

title: Sony to phase out "PlayStation Network" branding as early as this fall
summary: Sony plans to phase out 'PlayStation Network' branding this fall, citing evolving digital services.
insight: Branding change indicates shift towards broader digital service offerings.

title: Samsung could be developing a second-gen Galaxy Z TriFold and slidable handset
summary: Samsung is reportedly testing a second-generation Galaxy Z TriFold with improvements, despite initial discontinuation.
insight: Innovation continues despite initial product failure and market limitations.

title: Counter-Strike 2 update overhauls ammo system, punishes early reloads
summary: Counter-Strike 2's update overhauls ammo system to encourage caution in gameplay.
insight: Rewards strategic decision-making over impulsive gameplay tactics prevail.

title: Watch this restaurant robot malfunction and scatter tableware during live performance
summary: A restaurant robot malfunctioned during a live performance, scattering tableware. The incident quickly gained online attention, sparking debate about safety features and the use of robots in public settings. Consumers are questioning the wisdom of deploying robots in uncontrolled environments.
insight: Safety features of consumer-facing robots are insufficient for public use.

title: Walmart secures two AI pricing patents, raising dynamic pricing concerns
summary: Walmart secures AI pricing patents, sparking concerns over dynamic pricing practices.
insight: Lawmakers' efforts to restrict surge pricing may be undermined by Walmart's AI patents.
